title: leontodon carpetanus subsp. nevadensis
intro: Leontodon carpetanus subsp. nevadensis, also known as the Nevadan Hawkbit, is a subspecies of the Leontodon carpetanus plant, which belongs to the dandelion family and is native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Leontodon carpetanus subsp. nevadensis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of certain diseases. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and its leaves and roots can be used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
